visual_checks: Check the PDF's tab order settings in the page properties or document structure
Verify that the tab order is set to follow the document structure (tagged structure order)
If possible, test tabbing through form fields or interactive elements to confirm the order follows the logical reading sequence
Compare the visual layout reading order against the programmatic tab order
pass_conditions: Tab order is set to 'Use Document Structure' (document structure order) for all pages
Interactive elements receive focus in a logical order that matches the document's reading sequence
The tab order follows the tag tree structure of the PDF
fail_conditions: Tab order is set to 'Unspecified' which causes unpredictable navigation
Tab order is set to 'Use Row Order' or 'Use Column Order' instead of document structure
Tab order does not follow the logical reading sequence of the document
Different pages in the same document have inconsistent tab order settings
The document has interactive elements but no tab order has been defined
na_conditions: The document is not a PDF
The PDF contains no interactive elements, form fields, or links that would be navigated via tab
ne_conditions: The PDF tab order settings could not be determined from the available data
The PDF page properties could not be accessed for evaluation
common_mistakes: Leaving tab order at the default 'Unspecified' setting after creating a tagged PDF
Setting tab order to row or column order which may not match the logical reading sequence for complex layouts
Setting tab order correctly on some pages but not others within the same document
Assuming that tagging a PDF automatically sets the correct tab order without verifying the page property
Not updating tab order after reorganizing pages or content in the document

examples.pass: A tagged PDF form has its tab order set to 'Use Document Structure' on every page, and tabbing through fields follows the logical top-to-bottom reading order matching the tag tree
examples.fail: A PDF form has tab order set to 'Unspecified' causing a screen reader user to jump between fields in an unpredictable sequence unrelated to the visual layout
examples.partial: Most pages in a multi-page PDF have tab order set to document structure, but two pages still have tab order set to 'Unspecified'
